findings.evidence: PR line 278 proves `(0 : WeilDivisor X) ∈ degreeZeroSubgroup X`; Mathlib exports `zero_mem` for all zero-closed subobjects at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Submonoid/Defs.lean:71-77`.
findings.fix: Remove this declaration; users can write `zero_mem (degreeZeroSubgroup X)` or let simp use the existing `[simp]` `zero_mem`.
findings.issue: `zero_mem_degreeZeroSubgroup` duplicates the generic `zero_mem` API for every `AddSubgroup`.

findings.evidence: PR lines 281-291 define each lemma by immediately calling the same generic method; Mathlib provides additive subgroup closure via `AddSubmonoidClass`/`AddSubgroupClass` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Submonoid/Defs.lean:117-121` and `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Defs.lean:76-78,112-116`.
findings.fix: Delete these wrappers and use `(degreeZeroSubgroup X).add_mem`, `(degreeZeroSubgroup X).neg_mem`, and the existing subgroup `sub_mem` API at call sites.
findings.issue: `degreeZeroSubgroup.add_mem`, `degreeZeroSubgroup.neg_mem`, and `degreeZeroSubgroup.sub_mem` are only renamed wrappers around the existing AddSubgroup closure methods.

summary: The PR mostly reuses the existing Finsupp and kernel API, but it adds direct wrappers for generic AddSubgroup closure lemmas that should not become parallel TauCeti API.
